sentiment_score_definition: x <= -0.35: Bearish; -0.35 < x <= -0.15: Somewhat-Bearish; -0.15 < x < 0.15: Neutral; 0.15 <= x < 0.35: Somewhat_Bullish; x >= 0.35: Bullish
relevance_score_definition: 0 < x <= 1, with a higher score indicating higher relevance.

feed.title: A Look at Independence Realty Trust Inc (IRT) After 3.1% Gain -- GF Value $17.23 vs Price $16.32
feed.summary: Independence Realty Trust Inc (IRT) shares gained 3.1%, trading at $16.32, which is 5.3% below its GF Value™ of $17.23, indicating it is undervalued. The company holds a GF Score™ of 74/100, reflecting above-average overall performance, particularly strong in valuation but weaker in financial strength and growth. Insider activity shows no buying in the last three months.

feed.title: Phillips 66 (NYSE:PSX) Rating Increased to Strong-Buy at Zacks Research
feed.summary: Zacks Research has upgraded Phillips 66 (NYSE:PSX) from a "hold" to a "strong-buy" rating. This upgrade comes alongside several other analyst price target increases and positive news regarding asset acquisition and institutional interest. However, the company faces near-term earnings headwinds with a projected Q1 EPS loss.

feed.title: CSX (NASDAQ:CSX) Price Target Raised to $47.50
feed.summary: Rothschild & Co Redburn has increased its price target for CSX (NASDAQ:CSX) to $47.50 from $45.50, maintaining a "buy" rating and suggesting a 5.2% upside. The consensus rating for CSX is a "Moderate Buy" with a target price of $45.34, supported by 16 buy ratings and 9 hold ratings from analysts. Insider activity includes CEO Stephen F. Angel increasing his stake by 20.6% in March, while institutional investors own approximately 73.6% of the company's stock.

feed.title: Wells Fargo raises Booking Holdings stock price target on efficiency
feed.summary: Wells Fargo increased its price target for Booking Holdings (BKNG) to $215.00 from $214.00, maintaining an Equal Weight rating. The firm anticipates the company's Q2 nights guidance to be below expectations due to macroeconomic conditions, despite the company's strong operational efficiency, including an 87% gross profit margin. Booking Holdings reported better-than-expected Q1 2026 earnings but faces challenges from its 7% exposure to the Middle East.

feed.title: Cadence Design Systems, Inc. $CDNS Shares Bought by Mitsubishi UFJ Trust & Banking Corp
feed.summary: Mitsubishi UFJ Trust & Banking Corp increased its stake in Cadence Design Systems (CDNS) by 7.3% in Q4, acquiring an additional 12,427 shares. Other institutional investors also adjusted their positions in the software maker. The article also details CDNS's recent financial performance, analyst forecasts, stock performance metrics, and insider trading activity.

feed.title: Yum! Brands (YUM) Tops Q1 EPS by 13c
feed.summary: Yum! Brands (YUM) reported strong first-quarter results, exceeding analyst expectations for both earnings per share (EPS) and revenue. The company posted an EPS of $1.50 against an estimated $1.37, and revenue reached $2.06 billion, surpassing the consensus of $2.04 billion. Yum! Brands also reiterated its long-term growth algorithm, targeting 5% unit growth, 7% system sales growth, and at least 8% core operating profit growth.

feed.title: Garmin Ltd. (GRMN) Tops Q1 EPS by 27c
feed.summary: Garmin Ltd. (GRMN) reported strong first-quarter earnings, with an EPS of $2.08, surpassing analyst estimates by $0.27. The company also exceeded revenue expectations, bringing in $1.75 billion against a consensus of $1.72 billion.

feed.title: Taco Bell-parent Yum tops quarterly estimates as value deals fuel demand
feed.summary: Yum Brands, parent company of Taco Bell and KFC, exceeded Wall Street expectations for first-quarter sales and profit due to strong demand fueled by affordable meal offerings. While Taco Bell and KFC saw significant growth, Pizza Hut continued to struggle with declining comparable sales for a tenth consecutive quarter. The company's investments in AI-backed technology also contributed to operational efficiency and faster service.

feed.title: Yum Brands earnings top estimates, fueled by Taco Bell's 8% same-store sales growth
feed.summary: Yum Brands (YUM) exceeded Wall Street's expectations for its first-quarter earnings and revenue, largely driven by Taco Bell's strong performance, which saw an 8% increase in same-store sales. While KFC's U.S. business struggled, its international segment showed growth, and Pizza Hut experienced flat global same-store sales with stronger international results. The company did not provide an update on its strategic review for Pizza Hut.

feed.title: Yum! Brands (NYSE: YUM) Q1 2026 earnings rise as Taco Bell and KFC offset Pizza Hut
feed.summary: Yum! Brands reported strong first-quarter 2026 results, with GAAP diluted EPS up 72% to $1.55 and net income increasing 71% to $432 million. This growth was largely driven by robust performance from Taco Bell and KFC, which saw significant increases in system sales and operating profit. Pizza Hut's flat system sales and a 14% decline in operating profit were offset by the strong performance of the other brands, leading to an overall positive quarter for the company, boosted by increasing digital sales channels.

feed.title: IDEX (NYSE: IEX) posts Q1 2026 growth, record orders and lifts guidance
feed.summary: IDEX Corporation reported strong first-quarter 2026 results with net sales up 9% year-over-year, driven by organic growth and acquisitions. The company achieved record orders of $988.3 million and saw a 14% increase in adjusted diluted EPS. Consequently, IDEX has raised its full-year 2026 guidance for organic sales growth and adjusted diluted EPS, signaling strong confidence in its ongoing performance.

feed.title: Garmin (NYSE: GRMN) posts record Q1 2026 sales and EPS growth
feed.summary: Garmin Ltd. reported record-breaking first-quarter 2026 results, with revenue increasing 14% to approximately $1.75 billion and operating income growing 30% to $432 million. The company achieved GAAP diluted EPS of $2.09 and pro forma diluted EPS of $2.08, driven by strong performance in the fitness, aviation, and marine segments. Garmin also reaffirmed its full-year 2026 guidance for revenue and pro forma EPS and announced a recommended annual dividend of $4.20 per share.

feed.title: Ionis reports first quarter 2026 financial results and highlights progress on key programs
feed.summary: Ionis Pharmaceuticals reported strong first-quarter 2026 financial results, with total revenue increasing by 87% year-over-year, driven by commercial success of TRYNGOLZA and DAWNZERA, and substantial R&D revenue. The company increased its annual TRYNGOLZA peak net sales guidance to over $3 billion for severe hypertriglyceridemia (sHTG) and improved its 2026 financial outlook, anticipating two additional independent launches for olezarsen (sHTG) and zilganersen (Alexander disease). Ionis also highlighted progress in its partnered programs, including positive Phase 3 data for bepirovirsen in chronic hepatitis B and upcoming results for pelacarsen and eplontersen.

feed.title: Freight slowdown hits Old Dominion as shipments drop 7.9%
feed.summary: Old Dominion Freight Line (ODFL) reported a decrease in Q1 2026 earnings per diluted share to $1.14 from $1.19 year-over-year, with total revenue down 2.9%. The decline was primarily due to a 7.7% drop in LTL tons per day, largely driven by a 7.9% decrease in LTL shipments per day, although LTL revenue per hundredweight increased. Despite these challenges, the company highlighted improving demand as the quarter progressed and maintained strong service metrics, reiterating its long-term strategy for market share growth.

feed.title: VULCAN REPORTS FIRST QUARTER 2026 RESULTS
feed.summary: Vulcan Materials Company announced strong first-quarter 2026 results, reporting a 9% increase in Adjusted EBITDA and margin expansion, driven by widespread pricing growth and effective cost controls in its aggregates segment. The company reaffirmed its full-year Adjusted EBITDA outlook of $2.4 to $2.6 billion, citing momentum from large projects and public construction activity. Shareholder returns included $149 million in common stock repurchases and $68 million in dividends.

feed.title: Yum! Brands Reports First-Quarter Results
feed.summary: Yum! Brands announced strong first-quarter 2026 results, with worldwide system sales growing 6% and EPS excluding Special Items increasing by 15% year-over-year. Taco Bell showed exceptional performance with 8% same-store sales growth, while KFC demonstrated impressive unit growth and resilient same-store sales. Digital system sales reached a record 63% mix, underscoring the company's strong global appeal and technological capabilities.

feed.title: Expedia becomes IShowSpeed’s first travel partner as it enters livestreaming
feed.summary: Expedia has announced a yearlong partnership with the livestreaming personality IShowSpeed, marking the travel company's entry into the livestreaming space. The collaboration is set to begin with an extensive 12-hour live event spanning four Caribbean islands, highlighting Expedia's innovative approach to marketing through influencer collaborations. This move signals Expedia's strategy to reach new audiences by leveraging the popular livestreaming platform.

feed.title: HR and payroll company ADP revises 2026 outlook with Q3 results
feed.summary: ADP announced its third quarter fiscal 2026 financial results, reporting revenue of $5.9 billion and net earnings of $1.36 billion, alongside a revised fiscal 2026 outlook. The company also detailed key financial metrics including an adjusted EBIT of $1.8 billion and diluted EPS of $3.38. ADP will host a conference call and webcast to discuss these results further, with materials available on its investor relations website.

feed.title: Yum Beats Estimates as Taco Bell Offsets Tepid KFC, Pizza Hut
feed.summary: Yum! Brands Inc. exceeded sales expectations, primarily driven by strong performance from Taco Bell, which saw an 8% increase in same-store sales. This growth helped to compensate for weaker results from KFC, which grew by 2%, and Pizza Hut, which remained flat.

feed.title: IDEX posts record $988M in orders, raises 2026 profit outlook
feed.summary: IDEX Corporation reported a strong first quarter for 2026, with record orders of $988 million and a 9% increase in sales to $887 million. The company raised its full-year 2026 outlook, projecting organic sales growth to 3% to 4% and adjusted diluted EPS from $8.35 to $8.55, driven by strong performance in its Health & Science Technologies segment.

feed.title: Stronger Q1 results lift Generac (NYSE: GNRC) sales and margin outlook for 2026
feed.summary: Generac Holdings Inc. reported robust first-quarter 2026 results, with net sales increasing to $1.06 billion, driven largely by significant growth in the commercial and industrial segment, particularly due to data center demand. The company saw net income attributable to Generac rise to $73.3 million and adjusted EBITDA improve to $193.5 million. Based on this strong performance and a growing data center backlog, Generac has raised its full-year 2026 outlook for net sales growth and adjusted EBITDA margin.

feed.title: Sysco (NYSE: SYY) defends $29B Jetro buy while reaffirming 2026 guidance
feed.summary: Sysco CEO Kevin Hourican reaffirmed the company's full-year adjusted EPS guidance for 2026, expecting to hit the high end of the $4.50-$4.60 range, despite mixed quarterly results. He defended the $29 billion acquisition of Jetro Restaurant Depot, emphasizing its complementary channel, day-one accretive nature, and significant pro forma benefits in revenue, EBITDA, and free cash flow. Hourican also addressed investor concerns about increased leverage, outlining plans for rapid deleveraging based on the significant cash flow generation from the combined entity.

feed.title: General Dynamics Reports First-Quarter 2026 Financial Results
feed.summary: General Dynamics reported strong first-quarter 2026 financial results, with revenue up 10.3% to $13.5 billion and diluted EPS increasing 12% to $4.10. All four business segments showed growth, and the company generated $2.2 billion in cash from operating activities. Additionally, General Dynamics saw robust order activity with a consolidated book-to-bill ratio of 2-to-1, contributing to a total estimated contract value of $188.4 billion.

feed.title: General Dynamics pulled in $2.2B cash as quarterly orders hit $26.6B
feed.summary: General Dynamics (NYSE: GD) reported strong Q1 2026 financial results, with revenue rising 10.3% year-over-year to $13.5 billion and diluted EPS increasing 12% to $4.10. The company generated $2.2 billion in cash from operations, representing 192% of net earnings, and secured $26.6 billion in orders, resulting in a consolidated book-to-bill ratio of 2-to-1. Total estimated contract value stood at $188.4 billion, including a backlog of $130.8 billion.

feed.title: Phillips 66 Reports First-Quarter Results: Expanded Capacity and Continued Strong Operations
feed.summary: Phillips 66 reported first-quarter earnings of $207 million ($0.51 per share) and adjusted earnings of $200 million ($0.49 per share). The company increased its NGL fractionation and LPG export dock capacity, operated refining at 95% utilization, and increased its annualized quarterly dividend by 7%. Phillips 66 ended the quarter with approximately $6.0 billion in liquidity and is advancing significant projects like the Western Gateway Pipeline and Golden Triangle Polymers Project.

feed.title: WJ Wealth Loads Up on Emerging Markets, Snapping Up $6.1 Million in JEMA Shares
feed.summary: WJ Wealth Management has initiated a new position in JPMorgan ActiveBuilders Emerging Markets Equity ETF (JEMA), purchasing 113,984 shares valued at $6.1 million. This investment reflects a strategic move into emerging markets, with JEMA demonstrating strong performance, outperforming the S&P 500 significantly over the past year. The actively managed ETF offers both growth potential and a respectable dividend yield, making it an attractive option for diversified wealth management.

feed.title: ADP Reports Third Quarter Fiscal 2026 Results
feed.summary: ADP announced its third quarter fiscal 2026 financial results and a revised fiscal 2026 outlook. The company made this information available through an earnings release on its investor relations website and filed it with the SEC. ADP also hosted a conference call for financial analysts.

feed.title: GE HealthCare Cuts Outlook as Chip, Oil Prices Surge
feed.summary: GE HealthCare has lowered its annual profit outlook due to increased prices for memory chips, oil, and freight. The company now anticipates adjusted earnings of $4.80 to $5 per share, a reduction from its previous forecast of $4.95 to $5.15 per share. This adjustment comes despite the medical-technology company reporting a 7.4% rise in first-quarter total revenues to $5.13 billion, surpassing Wall Street expectations.

feed.title: Kenvue declares quarterly dividend of $0.2075 per share
feed.summary: Kenvue Inc. announced a quarterly dividend of $0.2075 per share, representing a 4.73% yield, with payment scheduled for May 27, 2026, to shareholders of record by May 13, 2026. The company has consistently raised its dividend for three consecutive years and its stock appears undervalued according to InvestingPro's analysis. This news follows a strong Q4 and FY25 performance with sales increasing by 3.2%, surpassing Street estimates, and several analysts raising their price targets for the stock.

feed.title: Pictet Asset Management Holding SA Trims Stock Holdings in PulteGroup, Inc. $PHM
feed.summary: Pictet Asset Management Holding SA reduced its stake in PulteGroup, Inc. by 11.1% in the fourth quarter of 2025, selling 66,461 shares and now owning 532,782 shares valued at $62.48 million. This comes as PulteGroup reported quarterly EPS of $1.79, slightly missing estimates, and revenue of $3.41 billion, down 12.4% year-over-year. The company recently announced a $1.50 billion share buyback program and a $0.26 quarterly dividend.

feed.title: REGENERON PHARMACEUTICALS ($REGN) Releases Q1 2026 Earnings
feed.summary: REGENERON PHARMACEUTICALS (REGN) reported strong Q1 2026 earnings, exceeding both EPS and revenue estimates. The company saw significant insider selling activity, while institutional investors showed mixed sentiment with both large additions and reductions in holdings. Congressional members, however, made purchases of REGN stock.

feed.title: Zacks Research Upgrades Copart (NASDAQ:CPRT) to Hold
feed.summary: Zacks Research has upgraded Copart (NASDAQ:CPRT) from a "strong sell" to a "hold" rating. This upgrade comes as the company's stock shows a market capitalization of $32.12 billion and a P/E ratio of 20.84, despite missing Q4 earnings estimates with $0.36 EPS against an expected $0.39. Insider selling by CEO Jeffrey Liaw was noted, but various institutional investors significantly increased their stakes in Copart during the last quarter.

feed.title: Johnson Controls International (JCI) Expected to Announce Quarterly Earnings on Wednesday
feed.summary: Johnson Controls International (JCI) is anticipated to announce its Q2 2026 earnings before the market opens on Wednesday, May 6th. Analysts are forecasting earnings of $1.11 per share and revenue of $6.0620 billion. The company last reported strong Q1 results, beating estimates with $0.89 EPS and $5.80 billion in revenue, and has issued guidance of $4.70-$4.70 EPS for FY 2026.

feed.title: Pictet Asset Management Holding SA Sells 328,267 Shares of IDEX Corporation $IEX
feed.summary: Pictet Asset Management Holding SA significantly reduced its stake in IDEX Corporation (NYSE:IEX) during the fourth quarter, selling 328,267 shares, which represents a 44.4% decrease in its position. Despite this sale, the fund still held 411,060 shares valued at $73,147,000. Other institutional investors showed mixed activity, with some increasing their holdings in IDEX.

feed.title: General Dynamics Corporation $GD Shares Purchased by Mitsubishi UFJ Trust & Banking Corp
feed.summary: Mitsubishi UFJ Trust & Banking Corp increased its stake in General Dynamics Corporation (NYSE:GD) by 5.1% in the fourth quarter, bringing its total holdings to 130,175 shares worth $43.825 million. Other institutional investors also modified their positions. The article highlights positive news for General Dynamics, including a $196.55 million contract and the U.S. Army proceeding with an ammunition plant, alongside upcoming Q1 earnings.

feed.title: Lennox (NYSE: LII) Q1 2026 revenue rises 6% as EPS slips 8%
feed.summary: Lennox International reported a 6% revenue increase to $1.1 billion in Q1 2026, driven by acquisitions, despite a 3% decline in GAAP operating income and an 8% drop in diluted EPS to $3.35. The Home Comfort Solutions segment saw a 10% revenue decrease, while Building Climate Solutions experienced a 38% revenue growth. The company reaffirmed its full-year 2026 EPS guidance of $23.50–$25.00 and free cash flow guidance of $750–$850 million, and updated revenue growth guidance to approximately 8%.

feed.title: Mitsubishi UFJ Trust & Banking Corp Acquires 35,981 Shares of VeriSign, Inc. $VRSN
feed.summary: Mitsubishi UFJ Trust & Banking Corp increased its stake in VeriSign, Inc. by 17.4% in the fourth quarter, acquiring an additional 35,981 shares. This brings their total holdings to 242,794 shares, valued at $58,987,000. Other hedge funds also modified their positions, with institutional investors now owning 92.90% of VeriSign's stock.

feed.title: Earnings Flash (GEHC) GE HealthCare Technologies Inc. Reports Q1 Revenue $5.13B, vs. FactSet Est of $5.03B
feed.summary: GE HealthCare Technologies Inc. (GEHC) reported its Q1 revenue of $5.13 billion, surpassing FactSet's estimate of $5.03 billion. However, their Q1 adjusted EPS came in at $0.99 per share, below FactSet's estimate of $1.05. The company also lowered its full-year 2026 adjusted EPS guidance to a range of $4.80 - $5.00, compared to FactSet's estimate of $5.06, leading to a pre-bell dip in share price.

feed.title: Enterprise Financial Services (NASDAQ:EFSC) Upgraded to "Moderate Buy" at Raymond James Financial
feed.summary: Raymond James Financial has upgraded Enterprise Financial Services (NASDAQ:EFSC) to a "Moderate Buy" rating, contributing to an overall consensus of "Moderate Buy" with a $66.00 price target. The company recently reported quarterly EPS of $1.31, exceeding estimates, though revenue for the quarter missed analyst expectations. EFSC shares opened at $59.38, with institutional investors and hedge funds owning 72.21% of the stock.

feed.title: Pictet Asset Management Holding SA Sells 1,101,598 Shares of Edison International $EIX
feed.summary: Pictet Asset Management Holding SA significantly reduced its stake in Edison International (NYSE: EIX) by selling 1,101,598 shares, now owning 965,928 shares valued at approximately $57.98 million. This decrease comes amidst mixed investor reactions to Edison's Q1 results, which beat EPS consensus but provided FY-2026 guidance slightly below Street expectations. The company also declared a quarterly dividend of $0.8775, translating to an annualized yield of about 5.2%.

feed.title: Expedia Group, Inc. $EXPE Holdings Trimmed by Mitsubishi UFJ Trust & Banking Corp
feed.summary: Mitsubishi UFJ Trust & Banking Corp significantly reduced its stake in Expedia Group, Inc. (NASDAQ:EXPE) by 38.1% in the fourth quarter, selling over 110,000 shares. This reduction positions their remaining holdings at approximately $50.8 million, while other institutional investors have shown varied activity, either increasing or initiating small positions. Additionally, an Expedia insider recently sold a substantial number of shares, and several analysts have updated their ratings and price targets for the company.

feed.title: Mitsubishi UFJ Trust & Banking Corp Cuts Stake in Edwards Lifesciences Corporation $EW
feed.summary: Mitsubishi UFJ Trust & Banking Corp significantly reduced its stake in Edwards Lifesciences Corporation (NYSE:EW) by 15.5% in the fourth quarter. The fund sold 125,516 shares, bringing its total ownership to 681,903 shares valued at $58.13 million. This comes as various other institutional investors have also adjusted their positions in the medical research company.

feed.title: Pictet Asset Management Holding SA Has $70.13 Million Stake in Packaging Corporation of America $PKG
feed.summary: Pictet Asset Management Holding SA reduced its stake in Packaging Corporation of America (NYSE:PKG) by 12.5% in Q4, holding 340,051 shares valued at $70.13 million. Despite this, other institutional investors significantly increased their holdings, and the company reported strong Q1 earnings, beating analyst estimates, and announced a quarterly dividend. Analysts maintain a "Moderate Buy" consensus rating for PKG, with a consensus price target of $229.86.

feed.title: CoStar Group, Inc. $CSGP Shares Acquired by Pictet Asset Management Holding SA
feed.summary: Pictet Asset Management Holding SA increased its stake in CoStar Group, Inc. (NASDAQ:CSGP) by 24.4% in the fourth quarter, bringing its total holdings to over 1 million shares valued at approximately $71 million. Several other institutional investors also modified their positions in CoStar Group. The company recently reported strong Q1 earnings, beating analyst estimates with $0.23 EPS and showing significant revenue growth, while also announcing a $1.50 billion stock repurchase program.

feed.title: Mitsubishi UFJ Trust & Banking Corp Has $49.35 Million Stake in Cintas Corporation $CTAS
feed.summary: Mitsubishi UFJ Trust & Banking Corp increased its stake in Cintas Corporation by 5.7% during the fourth quarter, bringing their total holdings to 262,376 shares worth $49.35 million. Several other institutional investors also adjusted their positions in Cintas, which currently has a "Hold" consensus rating from analysts with an average target price of $215.17. The company recently reported its earnings, meeting consensus estimates, and announced a quarterly dividend of $0.45 per share.

feed.title: Mitsubishi UFJ Trust & Banking Corp Sells 6,043 Shares of MSCI Inc $MSCI
feed.summary: Mitsubishi UFJ Trust & Banking Corp has reduced its stake in MSCI Inc by 5.9% in the fourth quarter, selling 6,043 shares and now owning 0.13% of the company. Other institutional investors have both increased and decreased their holdings in MSCI, while corporate insiders have also been active, with the CFO selling shares and the CEO purchasing a significant amount. Analysts maintain a "Buy" consensus rating for MSCI, with a target price of $692.70, and the company recently announced a quarterly dividend.

feed.title: Mitsubishi UFJ Trust & Banking Corp Sells 225,005 Shares of Colgate-Palmolive Company $CL
feed.summary: Mitsubishi UFJ Trust & Banking Corp significantly reduced its stake in Colgate-Palmolive Company (NYSE:CL) by selling 225,005 shares, decreasing its position by 21.1% in the fourth quarter. Other institutional investors, however, increased their holdings in the company. Colgate-Palmolive recently increased its quarterly dividend and reported earnings that surpassed analyst estimates.

feed.title: Mitsubishi UFJ Trust & Banking Corp Trims Stock Position in Blackstone Inc. $BX
feed.summary: Mitsubishi UFJ Trust & Banking Corp reduced its stake in Blackstone Inc. by 4.6% in the fourth quarter, selling 14,483 shares. This institutional activity comes amidst other firms adjusting their holdings and recent insider transactions. Analysts maintain a "Moderate Buy" rating for Blackstone, with a consensus price target of $150.95, even as the company recently announced a dividend cut.

feed.title: Mitsubishi UFJ Trust & Banking Corp Lowers Stock Holdings in Altria Group, Inc. $MO
feed.summary: Mitsubishi UFJ Trust & Banking Corp has reduced its stake in Altria Group, Inc. by 4.6% in the fourth quarter, selling 35,002 shares and holding 723,802 shares valued at $41.73 million. While some institutional investors increased their holdings, others have also adjusted their positions. Altria Group's stock opened at $67.76, with a market cap of $113.25 billion, and recently announced a quarterly dividend of $1.06.

feed.title: Mitsubishi UFJ Trust & Banking Corp Grows Stake in Marathon Petroleum Corporation $MPC
feed.summary: Mitsubishi UFJ Trust & Banking Corp significantly increased its holdings in Marathon Petroleum Corporation (NYSE:MPC) by 54.8% in Q4, acquiring an additional 98,651 shares to own a total of 278,734 shares valued at $45,331,000. This comes as other institutional investors also adjusted their positions, and several brokerages raised their price targets for MPC. The company recently reported strong quarterly earnings, beating analyst estimates, and announced a quarterly dividend.
